turns.text: Here's a photo of a coral reef I visited last month. It looked really healthy then.
turns.image_description: Underwater photo of a vibrant coral reef with coral in shades of orange, purple, and green, taken with natural lighting. Fish and marine life are clearly visible.

turns.text: This reef shows strong biodiversity with vivid coral pigmentation. The presence of fish and marine life suggests a healthy ecosystem. I'll keep this as our baseline for comparison.
I returned two weeks later and took this photo. The reef looks a bit different now.
turns.image_description: Same reef 2 weeks later, with 10% of corals showing white patches under similar lighting. Some areas have reduced marine life presence.

turns.text: I notice early signs of bleaching in about 10% of the corals. The reduced marine life presence suggests this stress is already affecting the ecosystem. Let's compare this with your most recent observation.
Here's the reef again, four weeks after the first visit. The conditions seem to have worsened.
turns.image_description: Reef 4 weeks later, with 40% bleaching and 20% coral death, taken during a cloudy dive session. Sediment plumes are visible.

turns.text: The bleaching has progressed significantly to 40%, with 20% coral mortality now visible. The sediment plumes and cloudy conditions might be exacerbating the stress on the remaining healthy corals. One more update to track the full trajectory.
This is the reef six weeks after my initial visit. The changes are quite dramatic.
turns.image_description: Reef 6 weeks later, with 70% bleaching, 30% coral death, and significant algal overgrowth. Strong currents are visible in the water.

turns.text:
What is the final state of the coral reef after the sequence of observations?

B) 70% bleaching, 30% coral death, and algal overgrowth
A) Vibrant reef with no visible bleaching
C) 40% bleaching with moderate marine life decline
D) 50% bleaching and 10% coral death with sediment plumes

ground_truth.reasoning_chain: Turn 1 established baseline health. Turn 2 identified early bleaching (10%). Turn 3 showed accelerated progression to 40% bleaching with 20% mortality and environmental stressors. Turn 4 revealed the full-scale degradation with 70% bleaching, 30% death, and algal overgrowth.
ground_truth.key_difficulty: The cumulative impact of bleaching and ecological succession requires temporal analysis to understand the full degradation trajectory.
